data.title: Vertiv Ramps Up Capacity To Feed Surging AI Data Center Frenzy
data.description:
Manufacturing Expansion In The Americas

The company will add or expand four facilities across South Carolina, Pennsylvania, and Mexico. These facilities will boost production of infrastructure solutions, power systems, and integrated cabinets.

The South Carolina sites are expected to increase regional capacity by approximately seven times. The Mexicali expansion is projected to lift capacity by about 45%.

Strategic Focus On AI Infrastructure

The expansion is aimed at accelerating deployment timelines and supporting scalable, high-density AI applications.

“Vertiv sees AI as a long-term, secular trend, and we are accelerating our capacity expansions to anticipate the continued growth in demand,” said CEO Giordano Albertazzi.

ThermoKey Acquisition

The transaction is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026, subject to approvals.

“Through our work with ThermoKey, we have come to value its differentiated heat-exchange technologies, engineering depth, and relationships across OEMs and system integrators. This acquisition is expected to expand the options available to our customers as they adopt more efficient cooling strategies and build infrastructure designed to stay ahead of rapidly evolving compute demands,” commented Albertazzi.

Technical Overview

Vertiv trades 1.6% below its 20-day SMA of $259.01. It remains 28.5% above its 100-day SMA of $198.36, maintaining a strong longer-term trend.

Shares have surged 173.24% over the past 12 months and trade closer to their highs. The stock is currently within a $53.60 to $276.78 52-week range.

RSI is at 55.10, in neutral territory, suggesting momentum has normalized after the mid-February overbought stretch. MACD is 10.3276 versus a 13.1067 signal line, a bearish configuration that often shows upside momentum is fading even if the broader uptrend remains intact.

With RSI above 50 and MACD still bearish, the setup points to near-term momentum leaning bearish, even as the bigger trend remains constructive.

  • Key Resistance: $255.50
  • Key Support: $234.50

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the April 22, 2026 (estimated) earnings report.

  • EPS Estimate: $1.00 (Up from 64 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $2.65 Billion (Up from $2.04 Billion YoY)
  • Valuation: P/E of 75.1x (Indicates premium valuation relative to peers)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $199.11. Recent analyst moves include:

  • RBC Capital: Outperform (Raises Target to $266.00) (Feb. 12)
  • Citigroup: Buy (Raises Target to $286.00) (Feb. 12)
  • Goldman Sachs: Buy (Raises Target to $277.00) (Feb. 12)

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Vertiv, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

The Verdict: Vertiv’s Benzinga Edge signal shows a High-Flyer setup with strong Momentum and Quality. However, the stock has an extremely weak Value profile. Pullbacks toward support may occur quickly if growth expectations weaken.

ETF Exposure

Significance: Because VRT carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows will likely trigger automatic buying or selling of the stock.

VRT Price Action: Vertiv Holdings shares were up 3.33% at $255.47 at the time of publication on Tuesday. The stock is approaching its 52-week high of $276.77, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: Stagflation Trade Is Back: Goldman Flags Inflation Risk As Growth Slows—ETFs To Navigate The Forecast
data.description:
A stagflation-like setup, in which inflation rises even as growth weakens, is beginning to take shape, and Goldman Sachs is anticipating it. The bank has raised its U.S. inflation forecast while lowering its growth outlook for this year, pointing to persistent energy-driven price pressures and rising downside risks to economic activity.

For markets, this is a difficult mix. Inflation erodes purchasing power, while slowing growth weighs on corporate earnings. So far, investors appear to be pricing in the inflation shock—but not fully the growth slowdown that could follow. That gap is where ETF strategies come into play.

Inflation Hedges: Commodities, Energy And TIPS Back In Focus

Goldman Sachs warns U.S. inflation can hit 4.9% by this spring if current disruptions persist. If this happens, it could hinder the Fed's strategic moves to bring inflation back to its 2% target. With energy prices being a major contributor to the inflation surprise, commodity ETFs are regaining traction.

Broad commodity funds that include crude oil exposure, as well as energy sector ETFs, directly benefit from supply chain issues and high energy costs.

Preparing For The Growth Slowdown: Defensives And Low-Volatility Plays

If Goldman's concerns about growth risks materializing turn out to be correct, then the cyclical parts of the market could be at risk. In fact, sectors such as industrials, consumer discretionary, and small-cap stocks are usually more vulnerable to an economic slowdown and may see their earnings estimates lowered.

Bottom Line

The forecast by Goldman Sachs points to a complex market regime where inflation remains elevated even as growth slows. For ETF investors, that means moving beyond traditional playbooks.

A balanced approach may be key: combining inflation hedges like commodities, energy, and gold ETFs with defensive equity strategies and selective fixed-income exposure. Funds such as XLE, GLD, and USMV illustrate how investors can position across both sides of the macro divide.

If inflation is the first phase of this cycle and growth slowdown the second, the opportunity lies in preparing for both.

Photo: SkazovD/Shutterstock

data.title: Coinbase Shares Are Sliding Tuesday: What's Driving The Action?
data.description:
• Coinbase Global shares are sliding. Why are COIN shares down?

Fresh Iran Threats Undercut De-Escalation Hopes

Israeli media also reported that an Iranian missile hit Tel Aviv, causing damage and minor injuries.

Geopolitical Risk Pressures Crypto Sentiment

That backdrop would likely weigh on Coinbase because COIN tends to trade as a high-beta proxy for crypto prices and overall retail risk sentiment. When geopolitical stress rises, investors often rotate away from speculative assets, which can pressure both Bitcoin and crypto-linked equities.

Bitcoin was also down slightly Tuesday, which adds to the weakness. Even a modest pullback in BTC can have an outsized effect on Coinbase shares because the stock often moves more sharply than the underlying cryptocurrency, especially when macro fear and risk-off sentiment hit markets at the same time.

Next Catalyst For COIN Stock Arrives With May Earnings Report

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the May 7 earnings report.

  • EPS Estimate: 47 cents (Down from $1.94 YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $1.58 Billion (Down from $2.03 Billion YoY)
  • Valuation: P/E of 45.1x (Indicates premium valuation relative to peers)

COIN Holds Buy Rating

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $287.39. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Baird: Neutral (Raises Target to $215.00) (Mar. 17)
  • Goldman Sachs: Buy (Lowers Target to $264.00) (Feb. 17)

COIN Shares Slide Tuesday Morning

COIN Price Action: Coinbase Global shares were down 8.69% at $183.16 at the time of publication on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: Crude Oil Gains Around 4%; US Business Activity Declines In March
data.description:
U.S. stocks traded mixed midway through trading, with the Dow Jones index gaining more than 50 points on Tuesday.

The Dow traded up 0.13% to 46,267.56 while the NASDAQ fell 0.49% to 21,839.82. The S&P 500 also fell, dropping, 0.05% to 6,577.63.

Check This Out: How To Earn $500 A Month From Goldman Sachs Stock Ahead Of Q4 Earnings

Leading and Lagging Sectors

Energy shares climbed by 2.5% on Tuesday.

In trading on Monday, communication services stocks fell by 1.2%.

Top Headline

The flash S&P Global composite PMI declined to 51.4 in March from 51.9 in February, recording its lowest reading since April 2025.

Equities Trading UP
           

Equities Trading DOWN

Commodities

In commodity news, oil traded up 3.9% to $91.56 while gold traded down 0.2% at $4,399.90.

Silver traded down 0.6% to $68.945 on Tuesday, while copper fell 0.8% to $5.4295.

Euro zone

European shares were mostly lower today. The eurozone's STOXX 600 fell 0.1%, while Spain's IBEX 35 Index fell 0.7%. London's FTSE 100 rose 0.2%, Germany's DAX declined 0.7% and France's CAC 40 fell 0.2% during the session.

Asia Pacific Markets

Asian markets closed higher on Tuesday, with Japan's Nikkei 225 gaining 1.43%, Hong Kong's Hang Seng index gaining 2.79%, China's Shanghai Composite rising 1.78% and India's BSE Sensex surging 1.89%.
